_Comment_ — `belongsTo` ticket, author. `is_internal` boolean + `attachments` array cast. Soft deletes.

_SlaPolicy_ — `belongsTo` organization. Global scope on `organization_id`. Integer casts for SLA minutes.

_ActivityLog_ — `belongsTo` ticket, actor. `meta` array cast. No timestamps (uses `created_at` with `useCurrent` from migration).

_User_ — updated fillable to include `organization_id` + `role`. Added `organization()` `BelongsTo` relationship.

---

<@U0BBL7EQZEK> — *Sprint 5 assignment: React frontend with Tailwind*

*Branch:* `feature/sprint-5-react-frontend` (already exists locally, 0 commits — start from there)
*Scope:* 4 issues, commit incrementally, open a PR to `main` when all are done.

---

*Issue 1 — Project Scaffold & Routing*
- Scaffold a new React app in `frontend/` (Vite + React). Install *Tailwind CSS* and *react-router-dom*.
- Set up React Router with these routes:
  - `/login` → Login
  - `/register` → Register
  - `/tickets` → Ticket List (protected route — redirect to `/login` if no token)
  - `/tickets/:id` → Ticket Detail (protected)

*Issue 2 — Auth Pages (`Login.jsx`, `Register.jsx`)*
- *Login* — POST to `/api/login` with `{email, password}`.
- *Register* — POST to `/api/register`. :warning: *The backend requires `organization_name` in addition to `name, email, password`.* Include that field in your form.
- On success, both endpoints return `{ token, user }`. Store `token` in `localStorage` and redirect to `/tickets`.
- Create a reusable auth helper that attaches `Authorization: Bearer {token}` to all API calls.

*Issue 3 — Ticket List (`Tickets.jsx`)*
- GET `/api/tickets` (Bearer token required).
- Filter drop-downs for *status* (`open, pending, on_hold, resolved, closed`) and *priority* (`low, medium, high, urgent`).
- Table columns: Subject, Status badge, Priority badge, Requester, Assignee.
- Clicking a row navigates to `/tickets/:id`.

*Issue 4 — Ticket Detail & Comments (`TicketDetail.jsx`)*
- GET `/api/tickets/:id` — show subject, description, metadata (status, priority, requester, assignee, created_at).
- Display threaded comments (the ticket response should include a `comments` relation — check the `TicketController@show` output shape).
- Comment form → POST `/api/tickets/:id/comments` with `{ body, is_internal }`.
- Add a toggle for *Public Reply* (`is_internal: false`) vs *Internal Note* (`is_internal: true`). Style internal notes distinctly (e.g., yellow/amber background).

---

*:warning: Two gotchas I verified in the codebase — handle these:*
1. *No `config/cors.php` exists.* The Vite dev server (typically `localhost:5173`) calling the Laravel API (`localhost:8000`) will hit CORS errors. Publish the CORS config (`php artisan config:publish cors`) and allow `localhost:5173`, *or* set up a Vite proxy in `vite.config.js` to forward `/api/*` to the Laravel backend.
2. *Register requires `organization_name`* — not just name/email/password. The backend `AuthController@register` validates and uses it to create the organization.

---

*Workflow rules*

> *<@U0BBL7EQZEK> — Sprint 6 work package. Branch, build, report, PR.*

*Branch:* Start fresh from `main`:
```bash
git checkout main && git pull origin main
git checkout -b feature/sprint-6-final-polish
```
*Commit style:* `feat(scope): description`, one logical commit per issue. *Validate every new PHP file with `php -l`* before committing. Post structured progress to <#C0BBUPKSJAF|agent-log> after each issue. Open a PR to `main`, post the link in <#C0BBUPM0PR9|human-review>, and *wait for human approval — do not merge*.

*Issue 1 — SLA Policies CRUD :warning: model + table ALREADY EXIST — do NOT re-create them*
The `sla_policies` table and `App\Models\SlaPolicy` (tenant-scoped, fillable, casts, `organization()` relation) are already on `main`. *You only need the controller + routes.*

1. Create *`backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/SlaPolicyController.php`* with full CRUD: `index, store, show, update, destroy`. Extend `App\Http\Controllers\Controller`.
2. Register routes in *`backend/routes/api.php`* inside the `auth:sanctum` group:
   ```php
   Route::apiResource('sla-policies', SlaPolicyController::class);
   ```
3. *Pitfalls (critical):*
   - On `store`/`update`, explicitly set `organization_id => auth()->user()->organization_id` — the `OrganizationScope` only filters *reads*, it does *not* auto-fill writes.
   - The table has a `unique(organization_id, priority)` constraint. Validate this in the request to avoid a raw `QueryException`: use `Rule::unique('sla_policies')->where(fn($q)=>$q->where('organization_id', $orgId))`, and `->ignore($policy->id)` on update.
   - Validate: `name` required string; `priority` in `['low','medium','high','urgent']`; `response_minutes` & `resolution_minutes` required integers `> 0`; `is_default` boolean. Use `['sometimes', ...]` on update.
   - _(Optional enhancement)_ If `is_default = true` on store/update, reset other rows in the same org to `false`. Not blocking, but nice.
4. *Verify:* `php -l` the controller; `php artisan route:list --path=api/sla-policies` shows all 5+ routes.

*Issue 2 — Dashboard Metrics (net-new)*
Add a stats endpoint returning: *ticket counts by status, counts by priority, SLA breach rate, average resolution time.*

1. Create *`backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/DashboardController.php`* with an `index()` method. Add route in `api.php` (sanctum group): `Route::get('/stats', [DashboardController::class, 'index']);`
2. All aggregates must be tenant-scoped — `Ticket` and `SlaPolicy` already apply `OrganizationScope`, so plain `Ticket::query()` is safe.
3. *Metric specs:*
   - *Counts by status / priority:* `selectRaw(status, count(*) ...)` group by the enum.
